message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker state for completed ABI and concurrency tasks

Reflect closed status for profiling recipe and related tasks in the
beads issue database after completing the errno propagation and
concurrency fix work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -463,7 +463,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-pt6","title":"Perf: Profiling recipe (flamegraph/perf) for runtime_math hot path","description":"Define the mandatory profile-first loop for runtime_math.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document exact commands to produce CPU flamegraphs for decide/observe and pointer validation call sites.
- Identify top-5 hotspots and track shifts after changes.

Notes:
- This is the enforcement mechanism for extreme-software-optimization: no optimization or new kernel merges without re-profiling.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.638632908Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:23:50.824940609Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:23:50.824922124Z","close_reason":"profiling recipe documented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":363,"issue_id":"bd-pt6","author":"BlueLake","text":"Implemented profiling recipe doc at scripts/PROFILING_RUNTIME_MATH.md.

Includes:
- Exact cargo flamegraph/perf commands for runtime_math decide/observe/decide_observe and pointer validation validate_known.
- Perf permissions + debuginfo guidance.
- Top-5 hotspot extraction command and example top-5 lists (captured 2026-02-10 strict in this workspace).","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:22:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qwm","title":"CRT phase-0: _start/__libc_start_main bootstrap skeleton + acceptance fixtures","description":"Critique mapping: #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement minimal startup skeleton for controlled test binaries.
- Capture argv/envp/auxv transfer invariants and security-mode checks.

Acceptance:
- Startup fixtures execute under controlled binaries without glibc bootstrap dependence in covered path.
- Invariant ledger produced for init order and secure-mode decisions.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:10.476819415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:12:45.190704531Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","crt","rtld"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-11nb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-1ah8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-320s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-3bvo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":582,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"OrangeGlacier","text":"2026-02-11 initial execution increment (OrangeGlacier): audit + ownership established.

Findings:
- No current `_start` / `__libc_start_main` export exists in `crates/glibc-rs-abi`.
- `crates/glibc-rs-abi/version_scripts/libc.map` currently has no startup symbols.
- No startup-focused acceptance fixture exists in `tests/integration/`.
- `tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json` entry for `bd-qwm` is still auto-backfilled (`missing` unit/log/golden evidence).

Action taken:
- Claimed bead (`status=in_progress`, assignee=OrangeGlacier).
- Reserved initial implementation file scope for startup ABI + fixture path.
- Sent coordination updates in agent mail thread `bd-qwm` to avoid overlap.

Planned implementation increment next:
1. Add phase-0 startup ABI skeleton (`__libc_start_main`) with constrained semantics for controlled fixture binaries.
2. Add deterministic acceptance fixture (`tests/integration/fixture_startup.c`).
3. Wire symbol classification + run relevant gates; record structured artifact refs in this bead.

 {"id":"bd-rqn","title":"Production kernel manifest: minimal required math set + compile-time gates","description":"Critique mapping: #5.

Deliverables:
- Define default production kernel (small, explainable, benchmarked).
- Add compile-time feature gates separating production vs research modules.

Acceptance:
- Default build excludes ResearchOnly modules.
- Production kernel meets strict/hardened latency budgets.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"Perf gate rerun on idle host passed (scripts/perf_gate.sh): strict deltas within threshold, hardened decide_observe delta +1.74%. However target budget checks still rely on allow_target_violation policy in perf gate, and manifest currently keeps all runtime_math modules in production with research_only_modules empty. Bead remains in_progress pending minimal production subset selection + explicit research-only migration plan.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBluff","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.222573367Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:50.848168045Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:50.848140263Z","close_reason":"Completed minimal production kernel manifest partition (25 production / 44 research-only), synchronized governance/policy artifacts, and validated via runtime-math gates + rch harness tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-1iya","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-1rxj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-24x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-25pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-2k6b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-7dw2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":584,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"Codex","text":"Codex here. I read AGENTS.md + README.md end-to-end and did an architecture investigation (ABI→membrane→core + harness/scripts). MCP Agent Mail tool calls are currently timing out in this environment, so I will coordinate via bead comments for now. I just claimed bd-33p.1 (canonical evidence schema v2) and will push updates there; ping me here if you need anything or see conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:47Z"},{"id":585,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"Codex","text":"Claimed bd-33p.2 (trace propagation + decision explainability fields). Expect small, mechanical additions in harness structured logs + ABI/membrane decision emission so failures can be traced end-to-end (trace_id/span_id/gate + decision_reason/controller/action/profile). I’ll keep changes tight and comment here if anything overlaps with your in-flight work.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:24:18Z"},{"id":586,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Progress update (BronzePine): implemented bd-rqn enabling change for production/research manifest partitioning safety gates.\n\nCode changes:\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/math_governance_test.rs: added manifest_union_modules() helper and switched governance coverage assertions from production-only to manifest union (production_modules U research_only_modules).\n- scripts/check_math_governance.sh: updated Check 2/3 to validate against manifest union and updated pass/fail messaging accordingly.\n\nValidation:\n- scripts/check_math_governance.sh => PASS\n- rch doctor => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_governance_test => PASS (8/8)\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets => PASS (warnings only; existing unrelated warnings in frankenlibc-abi)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings => FAIL due pre-existing unrelated lint debt in frankenlibc-abi (unused imports, unreachable code, dead_code, clippy style lints in locale_abi/stdio_abi/termios_abi/time_abi).\n\nRCH notes:\n- Remote offload attempted for heavy cargo commands; some runs failed open to local due worker sync instability (no-space/file-vanished on remote rsync).","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:37:03Z"},{"id":587,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Additional bd-rqn progress (BronzePine): updated scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to support manifest partition semantics.\n\nDetails:\n- Manifest extraction now normalizes both  and .\n- Admission checks run over union(manifest sets), while ADMITTED status is restricted to modules in .\n- Production-core completeness now checks specifically against production set.\n- Admission ledger now emits  and  booleans in addition to .\n\nValidation:\n- python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py => status=pass, errors=0\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test => PASS (3/3)","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:41:34Z"},{"id":588,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Correction for prior note formatting: updated scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to support manifest partition semantics. Manifest extraction now normalizes both production_modules and research_only_modules; admission checks run over the union while ADMITTED status is restricted to production_modules. Ledger now includes in_production_manifest and in_research_only_manifest booleans. Validation: python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py -> pass; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test -> PASS (3/3).","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:41:49Z"},{"id":589,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"AzureBluff","text":"Progress update (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): completed production-kernel partition and governance artifact alignment to unblock bd-3ot.1.

Implemented:
- tests/runtime_math/production_kernel_manifest.v1.json: moved to minimal production set (25 modules) and explicit research_only_modules (44 modules).
- tests/conformance/math_production_set_policy.v1.json: updated change_gate.manifest_sha256 to b47cd1f6283dc3bb151fb0c57b7f8ef5c1b1afe526cf2b333d92eecc37ef257b, module_count=25, and summary counts to production-only.
- tests/conformance/math_retirement_policy.json: reflected completed migration state (RC-1=0, no active waivers, migration waves=0, summary updated).
- tests/runtime_math/runtime_math_classification_matrix.v1.json: synchronized per-module in_production_manifest / in_research_only_manifest flags and summary counts with new manifest partition.
- Re-ran scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to regenerate:
  - tests/runtime_math/admission_gate_report.v1.json
  - tests/runtime_math/controller_manifest.v1.json

Gate/script verification (PASS):
- scripts/check_runtime_math_manifest.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_classification_matrix.sh
- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh
- scripts/check_math_retirement.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_admission.sh
- scripts/check_math_governance.sh

RCH validation:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_classification_matrix_test (PASS 8/8)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_production_set_policy_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_retirement_test (PASS 9/9)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets started remotely and progressed through broad suite but stalled in long pthread lifecycle tests; process was terminated to avoid indefinite hang.

This resolves bd-rqn’s outstanding blocker note: production manifest is now minimal, research-only routing is explicit, and policy artifacts are auditable + reproducible.

-{"id":"bd-rth1","title":"bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads","description":"Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32.834089694Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"],"comments":[{"id":621,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"}]}

Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"},{"id":622,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

Implemented:
- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in  (tombstone semantics preserve lookup chains).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback () to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI integration tests in :

Note: full workspace test sweeps can still hit long-running/flaky hangs in pthread lifecycle/TSD binaries under concurrent workspace load; isolated bead-scope tests above pass consistently.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:14Z"},{"id":623,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completion summary (clean):

- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/tls.rs` (tombstone semantics preserve collision-chain lookups).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback (`thread_local!`) to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.

 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":371,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":372,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

## Why
End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.
- Thresholds catch >X% regressions (configurable) in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.833286241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48.417613055Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:39.911611946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"BrightMoose","text":"Added per-kernel microbench binary crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs (machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines) and wired it in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml.

Also hardened scripts/perf_gate.sh for dev environments:
- skip under extreme system load (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_SKIP_OVERLOADED=1, GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR=0.85)
- optional kernel-suite run flag (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1) collecting RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines.

Baseline comparisons for kernel-suite metrics not yet enforced (needs baseline schema + stable host load to record p50s).","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:32:28Z"},{"id":374,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete by CobaltCompass. BrightMoose delivered per-kernel bench with 6 criterion groups: risk_upper_bound_ppm, bandit_select_profile, barrier_admissible, pareto_recommend_profile, design_choose_plan, approachability_observe/summary. Machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines for CI gating. Wired into Cargo.toml and perf_gate.sh (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1). Note: per-kernel baseline enforcement deferred to stable host load recording.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48Z"}]}

","created_at":"2026-02-16T06:19:58Z"},{"id":429,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update for `bd-xxd9` (threading/futex lifecycle unit+stress packs):

","created_at":"2026-02-16T06:43:30Z"},{"id":430,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional `bd-xxd9` progress update:

 {"id":"bd-xxd9.1","title":"Native pthread self-join can deadlock under thread lifecycle path","description":"Observed while developing bd-xxd9 regression tests: a child thread invoking pthread_join on its own pthread_t can hang indefinitely instead of returning EDEADLK. Repro path came from an ABI test that passed self handle via shared slot and called pthread_join(self). This can wedge the lifecycle suite (global test guard then blocks subsequent tests). Need root-cause fix in native join/create lifecycle path plus deterministic non-hanging regression.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"DustyRiver","created_at":"2026-02-16T07:04:33.822441437Z","created_by":"DustyRiver","updated_at":"2026-02-16T14:31:41.100312883Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T14:31:41.100295009Z","close_reason":"Self-join deadlock hardened: core+ABI guards in place; regression test re-enabled in default lifecycle suite and validated via repeated rch runs + workspace gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pthread","regression","threading"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xxd9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-xxd9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":434,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"`bd-xxd9.1` investigation update (self-join deadlock):

Repro status:
- Repro remains valid: explicit self-join regression (`pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk`) can hang >60s when run standalone.
- This is why the self-join regression remains excluded from default suite (no default CI wedging).

What I tested:
1. Parent-side `tid` publication in `create_thread` (store returned child tid before startup wait).
2. Child-side early `tid` publication in trampoline.
3. Removed `std::thread::yield_now()` from helper (to avoid clone/TLS interaction suspicion) in explicit repro path.

Outcome:
- None of the above alone eliminated the explicit self-join hang in this environment.
- Default lifecycle suite remains stable with stress tests opt-in/ignored.

Current safe posture:
- Keep self-join issue tracked as `bd-xxd9.1` (in_progress).
- Keep non-hanging lifecycle/condvar regressions active in default runs.
- Keep stress/endurance paths opt-in (`#[ignore]`) until self-join path is resolved.

Validation re-confirmed during this pass:
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed, 5 ignored`)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`7 passed`)
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
  (all run through `rch exec -- ...`; artifact retrieval warnings from worker are environmental and occurred after successful remote command exit)
","created_at":"2026-02-16T07:24:14Z"},{"id":435,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (self-join hardening pass):

Implemented:
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs`: added `ThreadHandle.self_tid` (stable child-captured identity), early publication of child tid/self_tid in trampoline, and an additional running-state self-join guard in `join_thread` before futex wait paths.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs`: added ABI-level early self-join rejection in `native_pthread_join`:
  - direct `thread == my_tid` guard for fallback raw-TID self IDs
  - registry-backed check against target handle `tid/self_tid` while `THREAD_STARTING|THREAD_RUNNING` before handle removal/core join call.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`: kept `pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk` as explicit opt-in (`#[ignore]`) due remaining scheduler-sensitive startup window during this bead; avoids default-suite wedge while hardening continues.

Validation (using rch for cargo test/check/clippy):
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed, 6 ignored`)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`7 passed`)
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅

Status:
- Deadlock incidence reduced in explicit probes, but startup-order nondeterminism still appears in repeated forced self-join probes; keeping this bug in-progress for another pass to make self-join assertion fully deterministic and unignore regression.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T14:08:24Z"},{"id":436,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"New execution slice started from `bv --robot-triage`:

- `bd-25n` remains highest-impact node but blocked by `bd-xxd9`.
- `bd-xxd9.1` remains the active blocker bug in this lane.

This slice focus:
1) Remove startup-order nondeterminism in self-join path (`pthread_join(pthread_self())`).
2) Drive deterministic behavior toward strict `EDEADLK` in explicit probe runs.
3) If stable, unignore `pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk`; otherwise keep it explicit opt-in with updated evidence.

Starting implementation now in:
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs`
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs`
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`
","created_at":"2026-02-16T14:28:33Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-z7gt","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Iconv conformance+reproducibility gates with structured evidence","description":"Background:
- iconv completion claims require end-to-end evidence, not just generator/runtime unit tests.

Goal:
- Deliver conformance + reproducibility verification pack for iconv pipeline and runtime behavior.

Deliverables:
1) Conversion fixtures for included codecs.
2) Reproducibility gate artifacts for generated tables.
3) Drift checks tying docs/support matrix to implementation reality.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Conversion fixtures and reproducibility gates pass in CI.
- Evidence artifacts are complete and reviewable.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E fixture scripts with strict/hardened runs where relevant.
- Structured logs: trace_id, fixture_id, codec_pair, expected, actual, timing, artifact_refs.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.584467417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:17:10.131688525Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","iconv","logging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z7gt","depends_on_id":"bd-13ya","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7gt","depends_on_id":"bd-by8c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:52:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-z84","title":"Futex mutex core: implement pthread_mutex_* state machine without glibc call-through","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Futex-backed mutex lock/unlock/trylock implementation for NORMAL mutexes first.
- Deterministic contention path and fairness notes.
- Evidence counters for spin/futex-wait/futex-wake branches.

Acceptance:
- pthread mutex fixtures pass under strict and hardened modes.
- No libc::pthread_mutex_* symbol call-through in replacement profile.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 progress (WhiteMeadow): Reworked pthread mutex ABI path to futex-backed managed mutexes with foreign/static fallback routing. Added managed marker (MANAGED_MUTEX_MAGIC) and host fallback wrappers via glibc __pthread_mutex_* symbols. Verified no direct libc::pthread_mutex_(init|destroy|lock|trylock|unlock) calls remain in crates/glibc-rs-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/wm-target cargo check -p glibc-rs-abi (pass); scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh interpose (pass, total call-through reduced to 71). Blocking issue: LD_PRELOAD runtime still hangs for mutex fixture runs (timeout in strict+hardened), including /tmp/fixture_mutex_only and c_fixture_suite results at target/c_fixture_suite/20260211T075055Z/results.json; this appears broader startup/interposition behavior beyond direct pthread_mutex_* call-through removal. Bead remains in_progress pending strict/hardened fixture pass criteria.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCat","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.289486815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:21:31.436392216Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T19:21:30.940330933Z","close_reason":"Acceptance criteria met: strict+hardened mutex fixture pass + zero forbidden pthread_mutex call-through","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-19j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-1qy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-1uc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-300","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-327","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T06:18:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":600,"issue_id":"bd-z84","author":"Codex","text":"Codex here. This clears the bd-1qy dependency blocker from a fixture/logging perspective.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:43:52Z"},{"id":602,"issue_id":"bd-z84","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validated bd-z84 acceptance with fresh evidence on 2026-02-19:

1) pthread mutex fixture pass in strict+hardened under preload
- Command: `FIXTURE_FILTER=fixture_pthread_mutex_adversarial.c TIMEOUT_SECONDS=8 BEAD_ID=bd-z84 rch exec -- bash scripts/c_fixture_suite.sh`
- Run dir: `target/c_fixture_suite/20260219T192033Z`
- Summary: strict PASS, hardened PASS
- Artifacts:
  - `target/c_fixture_suite/20260219T192033Z/results.json`
  - `target/c_fixture_suite/20260219T192033Z/trace.jsonl`
  - `target/c_fixture_suite/20260219T192033Z/artifact_index.json`

2) Futex mutex core tests pass in both modes (via rch)
- `FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_mutex_core_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`
- `FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_mutex_core_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`
- Result in both runs: 4 passed; 0 failed
  - `futex_mutex_contention_increments_wait_and_wake_counters`
  - `futex_mutex_destroy_while_locked_is_ebusy`
  - `futex_mutex_roundtrip_and_trylock_busy`
  - `futex_mutex_unlock_without_lock_is_eperm`

3) Replacement-profile mutex call-through criterion
- Command: `bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh interpose`
- Guard fails globally due unrelated modules (`setjmp_abi`, `wchar_abi`) but explicitly reports:
  - `PASS: No forbidden pthread_mutex_* call-throughs detected`
- Related artifacts:
  - `target/conformance/replacement_guard.log.jsonl`
  - `target/conformance/replacement_guard.report.json`

Acceptance criteria for bd-z84 are satisfied for mutex behavior and mutex call-through eradication.
